lines.text: Chandler and Joey's, Joey is balancing a mini hockey stick on his hand as Chandler enters from his room carrying a bunch of bills.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (watches Joey for a moment) Okay! (Joey quickly moves the hockey stick so that he's scratching his back with it.) Listen, I'm gonna be moving out so you will be in charge of paying the rent.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right! And when is that due?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: First of the month.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And that's every month?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, just the months you actually want to live here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahhh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, here is the phone bill. (Hands it to Joey.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking at it) Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's our phone number. Now look, I know I kinda sprung this whole me moving out on thing, so why don't I just-why don't I just cover you for a while?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no! No way! Joey Tribbiani does not take charity...anymore.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's not charity, Joe...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! Forget it! Okay-I mean thanks, but I'm done taking money from you. All right, I can take of myself. Now, what's next? Come on.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay uh, here's the electric bill. (Hands it to him.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is how much we pay for electric?!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, yeah.
Joey runs over and shuts off the lights.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, we'll do the rest of the bills later then?
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Chandler is talking to Phoebe about trying to give Joey some money.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So is Joey going to have to give up the apartment?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I hope not! I tried to offer him some money, but he wouldn't take it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, how much do you think he needs?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I figure that $1,500 would cover him for a few months, y'know? But I have to trick him into taking it so I won't hurt his pride.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why don't you hire him as an actor? You could have him dress up and put on little skits. Whatever you want.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well that would help the pride thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering from her room) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Wow! You look great! Wanna move in with me tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (thinks) Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! (They kiss) So, what do you girls have planned for tonight?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, instead of being sad that tonight is my last night together with Rachel we thought we'd go out to dinner and celebrate the fact that Rachel is moving in with Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And also, my birthday.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's not your birthday.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What a mean thing to say! I would never tell you it's not your birthday!
Joey and Ross enter.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! So you guys have anything planned for the big last night?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, instead of just hanging out, we figure we'd do nothing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa, Knicks season opener tonight. I thought maybe you guys would come over and watch it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know Ross, not if you're gonna talk about how you gave up a career in basketball to become a paleontologist.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I did give up a career in basketball to become a paleontologist!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering from her room) Pheebs, I was wondering...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're not dressed yet?! We're supposed to start having fun in 15 minutes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well and clearly not a minute sooner.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, you are packed though right, I mean please tell me that you're packed.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Of course I packed! Monica relax! I just wanted to ask Phoebe her opinion on what I should wear tonight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My God, I can't get a minute of peace around this place.
[Cut to Rachel's bedroom, Phoebe and her are entering. And it's obvious that she's not packed.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! Monica's just gonna kill you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, I know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, what you're wearing is fine for that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Rachel, I need to borrow-You're not packed!!!! You're not packed even a little bit!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Surprise!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no don't get mad because look-this is what happened. So I-I started packing, then I realized, "What am I doing? I am lousy at packing!" Right? But you love packing! So, as a gift to you, on our last night, ta-da!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (grabs a bag of those Styrofoam peanuts) I'll be coordinator! Oh my God! I'm so sorry, I didn't get you anything! Okay, look everybody has to help! Okay? You can help, can't you Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I have plans.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're plans were with us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, Chandler can make boxes, Ross can wrap, and Joey can lift things. Now Phoebe, go tell the guys they have to help out!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! (To Rachel) Oh my God, thank you!!
[Cut to living room, Phoebe enters and closes Rachel's door behind her.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hurry! Monica's gonna make you pack! She's got jobs for everyone! Now, it's too late for me, but save your selves! (The guys scramble for the door.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering and interrupting the guy's escape attempt) Okay! The movers will be here in 11 hours. Rachel has not packed. Now, everybody has to help! Chandler, we're gonna start with...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh nope, I-I have plans with Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I thought you said you were going to do nothing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but for the last time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay fine, now Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, but-but I can't do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (quietly) I've got Ben.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Because, because I've got Ben.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's almost 8 o'clock, it's almost past his bedtime. Where-where is he?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He's at a dinner party.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is he really coming? Because I can see right into your apartment!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Of course he is! What, do you think I'd just use my son as-as an excuse? What kind of father do you think I am?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, sorry. (Goes back to Rachel's room.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to the guys) I gotta go make a fake Ben.
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is entering carrying two pizzas.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here it is! Our last pizzas together as roommates.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I wish I'd know you were going to do that, I ordered Chinese.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh that's okay. Hey, actually in a way it's kinda nice. Me, bringing the food of my ancestors, you, the food of yours!
Chandler stares at him, dumbfounded, then finally agrees.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Say, Joe, I had a strange idea of what we could do for our last night. What do you say we play a little uh, foosball for money?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What, are you crazy? You haven't beaten me once since my injury plagued '97 season. It would be easier if you just give me your money.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes it would. What do you say to $50?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, you're on.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, let's play! The big game, Italy vs. China, apparently.
They start playing.
Rachel's bedroom, she's in her closet bringing down her pair of roller blades from a top shelf.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhhhhh, look it's the roller blades.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (starting to cry) Oh God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You remember when we got these?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I guess you weren't there. (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys, we said we were gonna have fun! Come on, hey, remember the time... (She starts laughing hysterically.) You don't remember?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry Pheebs, I guess I'm just really said that I'm leaving.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm gonna miss you so much.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, this doesn't have to be so sad though. Y'know? Maybe instead of just thinking about how much you're gonna miss each other, you should like think of the things you're not gonna miss.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't think there's anything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on, there's gotta be something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nope, she's perfect.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I have one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good! Great! You can go first.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh well, I guess I'm not gonna miss the fact that you're never allowed to move the phone pen. (Laughs. Monica lags behind the laugh a little bit.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good that's a good one. Okay, Monica, anything? Y'know? Does Rachel move the phone pen?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aw, sometimes. Always, actually.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, good. There you go. Doesn't anyone feel better?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not just the phone pen. I never get my messages.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You get your messages!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, well I don't think it really counts if you have to read them off the back of your hand after you fall asleep on the couch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So-so, you missed a message from who? Chandler or your mom? Or Chandler? Or your mom?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great! It worked! No one's sad.
Chandler and Joey's, they are just finishing up another game of foosball.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (scores) Yes! I win again! Ha-ha! That's like 500 bucks you owe me! Whoo-ho-hoo! (Goes over to the fridge and starts opening and closing the door rapidly.) $500 that is a loooot of electricity! (By the way, there's nothing in the fridge.) Whoo-ho-ho! (Notices the sparseness of the fridge.) I gotta buy some food.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, give me a chance to win my money back. Okay? Sudden death, one goal, $1,000.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You serious?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, get ready to owe me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, here we go. Ready?

[In slow motion, as some haunty demonic music plays in the background, Joey throws the ball in, Chandler quits playing and goes for his Chinese food. Joey smacks the ball really hard, shooting it down the table. Chandler slowly takes a bite, the ball bounces off of the wall, heads back up the table, and scores the goal for Chandler.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No! No! No-(Joey looks at him)-one can beat me.
In frustration, Joey kicks the table, breaking it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See? Now, that's why only the little fake men are supposed to do all the kicking.
Monica and Rachel's, the phone rings and Monica answers it as Rachel and Phoebe move a box into the living room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello? (Listens) Oh, hi Ross! (To Rachel) See? Other people call me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, your brother. Score!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (on phone) What's up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'm just over here with Ben. I thought we'd say hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, put him on!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ben, say hi to Aunt Monica. (He holds the phone to the fake Ben he has created out of a pumpkin.) Oh, I guess he doesn't feel like talking right now. He's smiling though! Okay, talk to you later.
Rachel's bedroom, Rachel is entering carrying two glasses of wine. She gives one to Phoebe, keeps one, and completely ignores Monica.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I think it was better when you guys were sad. Hey, uh, remember the roller blades?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know what else I'm not gonna miss? "I'm Monica. I wash the toilet 17 times a day. Even if people are on it!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: "Hi I'm Rachel, is my sweater too tight? No? Oh, I'd better wash it and shrink it!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: "I'm Monica, I don't get phone messages from interesting people. Ever!"

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! I call her!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: "Oh my God, I love Ross! I hate Ross! I love Ross! I hate Ross!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: "Oh my God, I can't find a boyfriend! So I guess I'll just stumble across the hall and sleep with the first guy I find in there!"
Monica picks up one of those boxes of hair curling things, dumps it in a box, and storms out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, we should get a move on if we wanna make those dinner reservations. (Phoebe dumps a drawer full of makeup into a box.)
Commercial Break
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is examining the broken foosball table as Chandler enters from his room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Still broken?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This sucks man! The last night you're here and I lose the two most important things in my life, the foosball table and $500.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, there are other ways of winning back your money, how about a little uh, a little Blackjack? (Holds up a deck of cards.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nah, not my game.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, uh, how about, how about-y'know what? We could play a new game. A new game, it's fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, what's it called?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Cups.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know how to play Cups.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll teach ya! Come on, come on, it's really easy and really, really fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, here you go. (He deals out two cards each.) I have two queens, what do you have?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A two and a five.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ho-ho, you win! 50 dollars!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah! Okay, let's play again. (He deals out two cards each again.) What do you got?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A four and a nine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're kidding right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well that's a full cup! (Pays him again.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Damn! I am good at Cups!
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is whining to Phoebe about Rachel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm not talking to her!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, one of you has to take the first step! And it should be you, because she's the one who's leaving. It's harder for her!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, maybe you're right-She made fun of my phone pen!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, I took it hard too.
Monica goes into Rachel's room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen Rachel, I feel really bad about-What are you doing? (She sees that Rachel is unpacking.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm unpacking!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm not moving! (She re-hangs a picture, crookedly.) Is that picture straight?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It needs to go about 20 blocks to the left!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, y'know what? You're the one who wants to make this big change and move in with Chandler! You should be the one to go! Why should I have to leave?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because it's my apartment!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well it's mine too! What else you got?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How about, you're moving!! (Grabs a bunch of clothes and throws them into a box. What follows is a brief sequence of Rachel unpacking and Monica packing the same stuff over and over again as Rachel chants "No." and Monica chants "Yes.")

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look! This is ridiculous. We should be packing you!! (She knocks over Monica, grabs a box, and runs into the kitchen.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, how are you guys doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great! Monica's moving!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) I am not!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh really?! Then how come all your stuff is in this box?! (Monica starts chasing Rachel around the table.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, you guys. You guys I think I know what's going on here. Okay, you guys... STOP!! (They stop.) I know that, I know that you're acting mad because you think that it'll make it easier to leave. But deep down you're still really sad. Deep-deep down.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No Phoebe I am mad!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, deep-deep-deep down!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I'm just mad!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Then keep running. (They resume the chase.)
Chandler and Joey's, they're still playing Cups.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You win.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, what did you have?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It doesn't matter because nothing beats a three and a six. That my friend is D-Cup. Okay, now much have you won so far?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, (counts the money) wow, 700.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not 700 exactly?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Double it! (He does.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well you see in Cups, once you get $700, you have to double it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, I didn't make up the rules. Now, after you receive the doubling bonus, you get uh, one card. Now that one card could be worth $100 bringing your total to 1,500. (Joey gets excited.) Don't get to excited because that's not gonna happen unless you get-No way! (He takes the top card, which is the two of clubs. Of course, any card would've won. Chandler pays him.)
Monica and Rachel's, Monica carries a box out of Rachel's room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, this is the last box of your clothes. I'm just gonna label it, "What were you thinking?"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Funny, because I was just gonna go across the hall and write that on Chandler.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey you guys, I don't mean to make things worse, but umm, I don't want to live with Rachel anymore.

lines.speaker: Monica and Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're just so mean to each other! And I don't want to end up like that with Rachel. I still like you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, Phoebe that's fine because I'm not moving.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa, Phoebe you gotta take her! Y'know I-I-I said some really bad stuff about her, but y'know Rachel has some good qualities that make her a good roommate.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh really? Like what Monica?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know she has 147,000 pairs of boots...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, good start Mon.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She lets you borrow them.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah and you stretch 'em out with your big old clown feet.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you wanna live outside?! Because it's gettin' cold! (To Phoebe) She gets tons of catalogs and umm, she'll fold down the pages of the things she thinks that I'd like.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What else?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: When I take a shower, she leaves me little notes on the mirror.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I do. I-I do, do that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's nice. I like having things to read in the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: When I fall asleep on the couch after reading, she covers me over with a blanket.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well y'know, I don't want you to be cold.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And when I told her that I was gonna be moving in with Chandler, she was really supportive. (To Rachel) (Starts to cry) You were so great. You made it so easy. And now you have to leave. And I have to live with a boy!! (They both break down in tears.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it's gonna be fine.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay Rachel, I can't wait to live with you! And you know what we should do? Bring Monica and then we could all live there together! We'll have so much fun!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But honey, I think she's moving in with Chandler.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh that's right. You're still set on that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Kinda. (She hugs Phoebe and looks out the window. She moves closer to it to get a better look.) Oh my God!
[Cut to Ross's apartment, he's watching TV and eating some popcorn as the phone rings.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (answering the phone) Hello? (Listens) Hey Mon, how's the packing going? (Listens) Ben? He's fine. Yeah, he's right-Oh my God! (He looks over at the fake Ben and notices that the head has fallen off.) Get your head of your shirt there son! (He tries to push the pumpkin through the neck hole.) What? (Listens) Yeah, it's a pumpkin. I'll come pack.
Chandler and Joey's, (Ohh, that's the last time I'm ever gonna type that line. It's just so sad.) Joey is entering, angrily.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross and I were helping the girls pack, took a little break, I lost $1,500 to him in Cups!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wh-How did you lose at Cups?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The same way you lost. I started out with a King and a Queen, bamn! Ross gets a 2 and a 3. Then I get a Jack and a King, boom! Ross gets a 4 and a 5! Ross was getting the Cup card, the D-Cup, the Sittin' Down Bonus! Meanwhile, I didn't even get half a cup! Nothin'!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh man!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And he never played before either! Y'know what I think? I think beginner's luck, very important in Cups.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, let's play one more hand! One more!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no more! I cannot lose another dime! I'm serious this time! In-in fact, look, there's a-I wanna give you something. And let me give it too you know before I pawn it for Cups money. (He rolls the big white dog over) Now, I want you to have the big white dog as a kinda of a, y'know, thank you for being such a great roommate.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't take the big white dog! You love it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's him, not it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, but wait-what if I bought it from you, y'know? And your nice gesture would be giving it to me at a reasonable price, say (Gets choked up) $1,500?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait a second, I see what you're trying to do here! You-you're trying to give me money again!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When did I try to give you money?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Over there! (Points to the couch) Before, with the bills! You tried to give some charity, I said "No," you dropped it. Okay? Then we had a nice last night together, we had some fun, we gambled, nobody tried to give anybody any money! Now out of the blue, you start with the charity thing again!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm just trying to help you out! Okay? I wanna make sure that you're okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I will be okay! Look Chandler, you gotta get it out of your head that I can't take care of myself. Okay? Look, I'm not gonna miss you helping me out with money. The only thing that I'm gonna miss...is you. And now the dog.

Monica and Rachel's, (And that's the last time for that line, no more Chandler and Joey's or Monica and Rachel's, ever!) Joey and Ross are carrying the last table of Rachel's. Rachel follows slowly, but is stopped by Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey. Call me when you get there. Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm really gonna miss you.
They hug.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! I have your key. Here you go. (Hands it to Monica.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.
Silence ensues.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God! This is silly, I'm gonna see you in a couple of hours! (They hug again.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bye house!
They break the embrace.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bye Mon.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye.
Monica closes the door and slowly walks into Rachel's old and now empty room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She really left.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know. (He kisses her.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No problem roomie. (She turns around and hugs him.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Can I ask you a question?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What the hell is that dog doing here?! (She notices the dog sitting in the living room.)
And with that, an era ends as Chandler moves in with Monica as Rachel moves in with Phoebe. It 'tis a sad and happy time for Friends.
Ending Credits
Ross's apartment, Chandler is trying to get Joey's money back from Ross.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I invented the game of Cups as a way to give Joey money.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And now you want that money back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler, what kind of an idiot do you take me for? (As he picks up the fake Ben.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's not a real game! I made it up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry you lost your money, but I won it fair and square.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: At a fake game!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now, if you wanna try to make some of it back, I'd be glad to play you for it. But I should warn you, I am very good at Cups.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! Now I assume the Saucer card came up when you played last.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hmm, let's see if it comes up this time. (He looks at his cards and shrugs.)
End
